campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's financial report for March 2026 and discussed a new policy restricting how the city's property can be used in connection with federal civil enforcement activities. The council also addressed current city job openings and strategies to hire and keep employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority March 2026 investment report
New policy prohibiting city property use for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and employee recruitment and retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties that the county identified. They also adopted a new technology policy for the city and approved a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Hazardous property abatement on county-identified sites
New City Council Technology Disruption Policy
Procedure for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review the town's finances for March, and authorize several spending and contract decisions. Key approvals included accepting a donated automated external defibrillator, signing a five-year service agreement, awarding a paving contract to O'Grady Paving, and submitting grant applications.
keyTopics: Approved O'Grady Paving construction contract
Accepted donation of automated external defibrillator (AED)
Reviewed March 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized five-year services agreement
Submitted grant applications for statewide projects

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting on May 20, 2026 to organize upcoming business. They cancelled their May 26 meeting and reviewed the draft agenda for June 2, deciding which items to add and whether any need special time allocations.
keyTopics: May 26 council meeting cancelled
June 2 draft agenda items under review
Updates to Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan
Council-sponsored events approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council selected and recommended new members for the Youth Advisory Committee for fiscal year 2026-27.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee membership selection for 2026-27
Public comment period on city matters
Upcoming agendas for boards, commissions, and committees

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on May 19, 2026, where they recognized Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month. The meeting included approval of April meeting minutes and standard council procedures.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month designation
April meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's Health and Safety Element—a long-range planning document covering 2015-2040—and celebrated Public Works Week by recognizing the departments that maintain roads, sidewalks, parks, and city facilities. The council also recognized Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Public Works Department divisions including streets, sidewalks, trees, and facilities
Public Works Week celebration (May 17-23, 2026)
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month recognition

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara Station Area Task Force held a special meeting on May 21, 2026, to discuss plans and developments related to the station area. The meeting was open to the public both in person at City Hall and online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting convened
Meeting held at City Hall, 1500 Warburton Avenue
Remote participation available via Zoom

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held several public hearings on May 19, 2026, to consider new city rules, approve a land development permit change, confirm a property assessment, and permanently appoint a new City Manager.
keyTopics: New urgency ordinance passed to add regulations quickly
Site development permit amendment and tentative map approved for a property project
Property assessment confirmed and ordered
Permanent City Manager appointment approved

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council discussed plans to help residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances in their homes. The city is developing new principles and programs to support this transition while meeting air quality requirements set by regional regulators.
keyTopics: Residential electrification program guidelines and principles
Compliance with Air District regulations
Support programs to help residents transition away from gas appliances
Status update on city's electrification efforts
